chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.D-478 of 2025 “Khuda Bux Vs. Province of Sindh and others” DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 17.12.2025 Mr. Muhammad Ayoub Chaniho, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, Assistant A.G. Sindh. = Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that the petitioner is a resident of Village Haji Banko Chaniho, Union Council No. V, Banko Chaniho, Taluka Khipro, District Sanghar. It was contended that the access road leading to the petitioner’s village has been constructed only up to a length of 15 kilometers, whereas the remaining 5 kilometers of the road have been completely damaged due to heavy rainfall. As a result, the residents of the area are facing serious difficulties in accessing their village. Learned counsel, therefore, prayed that the present petition be allowed. Learned Assistant Advocate General filed a report stating that a detailed survey was conducted, which revealed that the link road measures 3.120 kilometers. The survey report was submitted vide letter No. DB/G-189/241 dated 05.03.2025. It was further submitted that the said road was damaged due to heavy rainfall in the year 2022 and requires major repairs. The field staff, in coordination with the local residents, conducted the survey and prepared a feasibility report for approval by the competent authority. All relevant documents were also provided to the petitioner by the concerned authority for his information. At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner sought disposal of the petition in terms of the feasibility report submitted by the Works and Services Department, Government of Sindh.

All relevant documents were also provided to the petitioner by the concerned authority for his information. At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner sought disposal of the petition in terms of the feasibility report submitted by the Works and Services Department, Government of Sindh. 2 In view of the above, and with the consent of both parties, the petition is disposed of with a direction to respondent No. 2 to take necessary steps in accordance with the feasibility report. JUDGE JUDGE *Faisal*
